body.title: [dependency] No-builders-available alarm: rekey, clear-on-builder-online, re-poll not park
body.intention: I intend that when no capable builder is available, Granny raises a correctly-keyed 'No builders available' singleton alarm that increments on each 10-min re-check, keeps re-polling instead of parking, and clears itself the moment a builder comes online — so the gap is visible and self-healing, not a dead-parked daemon.
body.description:
PHASE 2 (of D-granny-pull-dispatch-model). Reconciliation verdict PARTIAL: the 2026-07-02 alarm is raise_alarm('granny-stalled', ...daemon, 'I'M STALLED! HELP! no wakeable/available worker...') at daemon.py:923-927, which then set_stalled() and PARKS Granny (run_once early-returns while stalled, daemon.py:757-759). raise_alarm is already singleton-by-signature with a counter (system_alarms.py:206-231), so that property holds. Gaps: (1) rekey from generic 'granny-stalled' to the issue-specific 'no-builders-available' (distinguish from other stalls); (2) Granny NEVER calls close_alarm (grep: zero callers) — add a clear when a builder comes online; (3) replace park-until-resume with a 10-min re-poll cadence so the alarm re-increments while still checking. Use the class/level from T-availability-semaphore-build-class to raise the 'found but insufficient level' variant. Structured failure-time diagnostics on this alarm are T-self-diagnostic-trouble-tickets' job — this ticket wires the lifecycle (key/clear/cadence), that ticket fills the evidence payload.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/granny/daemon.py (rekey, close_alarm-on-builder-online, re-poll cadence vs park), unseen_university/system_alarms.py (only if close_alarm needs a caller-friendly wrapper).
**Design rules:** log-every-state-change (alarm raise/clear are state changes — INFO); external-state (alarm record persists in the flat-file store).
**Scope boundary:** IN — alarm key, clear-on-builder-online, re-poll cadence (not park). OUT — the structured diagnostics payload (T-self-diagnostic-trouble-tickets), the availability class encoding it reads (T-availability-semaphore-build-class, gated).
**Test plan:** no-builder condition raises 'no-builders-available' (red: 'granny-stalled'/none; green: correct key); a builder coming online clears it (red: stays open; green: close_alarm fired, record cleared); Granny re-polls on cadence rather than parking (red: parked/early-return forever; green: re-check every ~10 min, counter increments).
**Completion criteria:** alarm is issue-keyed, auto-clears when a builder appears, and Granny keeps re-polling on a 10-min cadence instead of parking.
**Rollback:** revert to the park-on-granny-stalled behavior.
